Export paginated API data to a local file. Supports JSONL (one JSON object
per line, streaming-friendly) and JSON (array). JSONL is recommended for
large datasets as it has no memory pressure.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> [id] [flags]

Examples:
  # Export all items as JSONL (streaming, recommended for large datasets)
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl --output data.jsonl

  # Export with limit
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl --limit 1000

  # Pipe to another tool
  everbee-pp-cli export <resource> --format jsonl | jq '.id'

Seeded keyword search.

Usage:

Examples:

Flags:
      --count-search             Count this search against the account's plan quota, matching EverBee UI behavior. (default true)
  -h, --help                     help for search
      --keyword string           Seed keyword to research, e.g. 'dad shirt'. Comma-join multiple seeds: 'dad shirt,fishing dad shirt'.
      --order-by string          Sort field: new_volume (search volume), competition, or score. (default "new_volume")
      --order-direction string   Sort direction: desc or asc. (default "desc")
      --page string              Result page (20 keywords per page). (default "1")
      --stdin                    Read request body as JSON from stdin
      --type-of-search string    EverBee keyword search mode. keywords_suggestion returns related-keyword suggestions for the seed. (default "keywords_suggestion")

Lists rows from the learn_candidates table: corrections and playbook
shapes the CLI derived from observed usage. Candidates are quarantined
until an explicit 'learnings confirm <id>' materializes them; they never
alter command behavior or recall's verified results on their own.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli learnings candidates [flags]

Examples:
  everbee-pp-cli learnings candidates
  everbee-pp-cli learnings candidates --class flag_alias
  everbee-pp-cli learnings candidates --status rejected

Flags:
      --class string    Filter by class (flag_alias | playbook_candidate)
      --db string       SQLite database file path (default: resolved data directory data.db)

Promotes one open candidate into the verified knowledge tables.

The full candidate payload is printed before anything is written, so
the caller always sees exactly what is being blessed. Materialization
is per class:

  playbook_candidate  upserts a playbook row for the candidate's query
                      family (confidence 2, the same floor as a teach)
  flag_alias          appends a timestamped [amend ...] correction note
                      to the query family's playbook notes

Removes rows from the search_learnings table so a bad teach can be
undone without dropping the whole DB.

Requires at least one of --resource, --action, or --all.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli learnings forget <query> [flags]

Examples:
  everbee-pp-cli learnings forget "<question>" --resource <id>
  everbee-pp-cli learnings forget "<question>" --all

      --action string     Delete only rules with this action (boost | hide | alias_of)
      --all               Delete every rule for the supplied query
      --db string         SQLite database file path (default: resolved data directory data.db)
  -h, --help              help for forget
      --resource string   Delete only the rule for this resource_id

Search EverBee's Etsy listing database for a term.

This is the endpoint EverBee's Product Analytics search box calls. It is the
answer to "what listings exist for this niche" — unlike 'products list', which
browses EverBee's default feed and ignores any term you give it.

Each listing carries estimated monthly sales and revenue, price, tags, listing
type (physical vs digital download), listing age, conversion rate, and its shop.
Rows are returned as EverBee ranked them; for relevance-annotated, evidence-scored
output use 'research niche' instead.

Compare a niche against a saved baseline and report what actually moved.

EverBee exposes no history at all, so week-over-week movement is only knowable
from snapshots kept locally. Run with --save-baseline to record the current
verdict, then re-run later to see the deltas in demand, competition, opportunity,
and median price. Both fetch timestamps and the comparison window travel with the
result, so the period being compared is never ambiguous.

Resolve an Etsy listing URL or ID against the local research store.

EverBee exposes no listing-detail endpoint, so a listing's identity is resolved
against the listings already synced or researched locally. That makes the honest
answer three-valued, and this command reports which one applies:

  - unresolved: the input is not a recognizable Etsy listing identity
  - resolved, no data: a real listing ID, but nothing has been researched about it
  - resolved, with data: the listing and its metrics

The middle case is the one that matters. The published CLI reported it as an empty
result, which reads as "this listing is worthless" when the truth is "we have not
looked yet". Here it comes back with resolved=true, has_data=false, and the sync

Score an Etsy niche from a seed keyword and return the evidence behind the score.

Runs EverBee's seeded keyword search and seeded product search for the seed, then
scores demand, competition, saturation, price band, and an opportunity score
locally. Every returned row is annotated with its relevance to the seed and every
metric carries its provenance, so a "low competition" claim can be audited rather
than trusted. Confidence tracks evidence coverage: it is capped when there is no
keyword evidence and is 0 when nothing relevant came back.

Rows are never dropped. Use --min-relevance to set the floor a row must clear to
count as evidence; rows below it are still returned, marked with their score.

Use this command for a single-seed niche verdict with evidence. Do NOT use it to
enumerate child niches; use 'research subniches' instead. For market-shape stats
alone, use 'research competitors'.

Verify that the research path is semantically sound, not merely reachable.

An HTTP 200 proves the transport works. It does not prove the answer means
anything: EverBee's default suggestion feeds return 200 with unranked filler that
has nothing to do with the query, and mistaking that for evidence is exactly what
made the published CLI report confident nonsense.

This command runs a canonical seed through the live seeded endpoints and asserts
the properties that make an answer trustworthy: results come back, they are
actually about the seed, EverBee supplied the seed's own demand/competition
metrics, and the account has research quota left.

Exit codes:
  0  the research path is sound
  3  degraded — transport works but the data is not trustworthy
  4  failed — the research path is broken or the plan quota is exhausted

Run this first in any automated session.

Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. This API does
not declare temporal sync filters, so sync performs full pagination unless an
explicit resource declares its own incremental filter.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

Resource scoping:
  --resources runs the named top-level resources, plus any parent-keyed
  dependent whose own name is listed OR whose parent table is listed.
  Naming a parent therefore cascades to its dependents (so "sync this
  parent and its children" works without listing every nested resource
  by hand). There is no flag today to suppress the cascade for a named
  parent. To run a dependent without re-syncing its parent, list only
  the dependent by name; the parent table must already be populated
  from a prior sync.

Usage:
  everbee-pp-cli sync [flags]

Examples:
  # Sync all resources
  everbee-pp-cli sync

  # Sync specific resources only
  everbee-pp-cli sync --resources channels,messages

  # Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  everbee-pp-cli sync --full

  # Incremental sync: only records from the last 7 days
  everbee-pp-cli sync --since 7d

  # Parallel sync with 8 workers
  everbee-pp-cli sync --concurrency 8

  # Latest-only: refresh head of each resource, no historical backfill
  everbee-pp-cli sync --latest-only

Flags:
      --concurrency int              Number of parallel sync workers (default 4)
      --db string                    SQLite database file path (default: resolved data directory data.db)
      --full                         Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
      --global-param stringArray     Extra query param to inject into every sync request including dependent path-scoped calls (repeatable, key=value). Use when an API requires a scope on every call regardless of path nesting.
  -h, --help                         help for sync
      --latest-only                  Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins).
      --max-pages int                Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event)
      --no-prune                     Disable deletion reconciliation on --full (by default a full sync prunes local rows the API no longer returns for a fully-enumerated parent partition)
      --param stringArray            Extra query param to inject into flat-list sync requests (repeatable, key=value). Skipped on path-scoped dependent requests so a top-level scope like workspace=<id> does not double up on /parents/<id>/children calls. Use --global-param to inject everywhere. Avoid pagination keys (limit/since/cursor) — overriding them corrupts resume state.
      --resource-param stringArray   Per-resource extra query param (repeatable, resource:key=value). Wins over --param and --global-param when keys conflict.
      --resources strings            Comma-separated resource types to sync. Naming a parent also runs its parent-keyed dependents (see Long help for scoping).
      --since string                 Incremental sync duration (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w, 30m)
      --strict                       Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero).

This command is designed to be backgrounded by an LLM right before it
emits the user-facing response: silent on success, errors only to
the CLI state directory's teach.log, safe to fire-and-forget.
